      "file": "packages/app-store/index.ts",

      "title": "appStore exports Promise objects instead of modules",
      "description": "The appStore object was changed to use dynamic imports (which return Promises) but the export and usage pattern doesn't await these Promises. Every property like `appStore.applecalendar` is now a Promise<module> rather than the actual module. Code calling `appStore[key]` expecting synchronous module access will receive unresolved Promises, causing type errors and runtime failures when accessing properties like `.lib.CalendarService`.",
      "suggestion": "Either: (1) Create a factory function that awaits all imports and returns the resolved modules, (2) Change all call sites to properly await appStore lookups, or (3) Use a lazy-loading pattern with a getter/cache mechanism. The current approach breaks the contract of the appStore export."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "type-mismatch",
      "file": "packages/core/CalendarManager.ts",
      "line": 47,
      "title": "getCalendar() now returns Promise but synchronous assignment doesn't await",
      "description": "In getCalendarCredentials function, `const calendar = getCalendar(credential)` assigns a Promise to `calendar`, but the code then checks `return app.variant === \"calendar\" ? [{ integration: app, credential, calendar }]` and passes `calendar` as if it's already resolved. This causes the object structure to contain unresolved Promises instead of Calendar instances.",
      "suggestion": "Either make getCalendarCredentials async and await the getCalendar call, or modify the data structure to store promises and resolve them at consumption points (like in getConnectedCalendars where calendar is now correctly awaited)."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "incomplete-refactor",
      "file": "packages/core/CalendarManager.ts",
      "line": 141,
      "title": "getCachedResults mixes sync and async calendar retrieval",
      "description": "Line 141 uses `Promise.all(calendarCredentials.map((credential) => getCalendar(credential)))` to await calendars, but the variable `calendars` is then used in a `.map()` loop expecting Calendar instances. However, some Calendar instances may be null (from failed imports), and the subsequent code at line 142 tries to call methods on potentially unresolved values.",
      "suggestion": "Ensure proper null-checking and verify that all null values are filtered out before the async operations loop. The Promise.all is correct, but downstream code must handle potential nulls from failed getCalendar calls."
    }
  ]
}
